records.ety: [F. chevalerie, fr. chevalier knight, OF., horseman. See Chevalier, and cf. Cavalry.]

defs: A body or order of cavaliers or knights serving on horseback; illustrious warriors, collectively; cavalry.
quotes: By his light Did all the chivalry of England move, To do brave acts.

defs: The dignity or system of knighthood; the spirit, usages, or manners of knighthood; the practice of knight-errantry.
The qualifications or character of knights, as valor, dexterity in arms, courtesy, etc.
quotes: The glory of our Troy this day doth lie On his fair worth and single chivalry.

defs: A tenure of lands by knight's service; that is, by the condition of a knight's performing service on horseback, or of performing some noble or military service to his lord.
